events.title: George Michael would have been 63
events.blurb: Legendary singer-songwriter George Michael, born in London on June 25, 1963, would have turned 63.

angles.name: Remembering George Michael
angles.copy: Remembering the incredible talent of George Michael, who would have been 63 today. His music continues to inspire and move us.

events.title: 17 years since Michael Jackson's passing
events.blurb: June 25, 2026, marks 17 years since the world lost the King of Pop, Michael Jackson, who died on June 25, 2009.

angles.name: King of Pop Tribute
angles.copy: 17 years ago, we said goodbye to the King of Pop, Michael Jackson. His music and artistry remain timeless.

events.title: Ringo Starr turns 86
events.blurb: The legendary Beatles drummer, Ringo Starr, celebrates his 86th birthday today, having been born on July 7, 1940.

angles.name: Happy Birthday Ringo
angles.copy: Happy 86th Birthday to the one and only Ringo Starr! Still bringing joy and good vibes to the world.

events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's iconic guitarist, Brian May, celebrates his 79th birthday today, born on July 19, 1947.

angles.name: Happy Birthday Brian May
angles.copy: Wishing a very Happy 79th Birthday to the legendary Sir Brian May! Still rocking the world with that iconic guitar.

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: The Rolling Stones' legendary frontman, Mick Jagger, celebrates his 83rd birthday today, born on July 26, 1943.

angles.name: Happy Birthday Mick Jagger
angles.copy: Still got the moves! Happy 83rd Birthday to the one and only Mick Jagger. What an absolute legend.

events.title: 45 years since MTV launched
events.blurb: On August 1, 1981, MTV launched with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' as its first video, marking 45 years today since it changed music television forever.

angles.name: MTV Changed Everything
angles.copy: 45 years ago today, MTV launched and changed the music world forever. The irony of its first video, 'Video Killed The Radio Star,' is still brilliant.

events.title: Oasis' 'Definitely Maybe' turns 32
events.blurb: Oasis released their iconic debut album, 'Definitely Maybe,' on August 29, 1994, which turns 32 today.

angles.name: Definitely Maybe Anniversary
angles.copy: 32 years of 'Definitely Maybe'! Oasis's debut album still sounds as fresh and vital today as it did in '94. What's your favourite track?

events.title: 57 years since Woodstock
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Festival, a pivotal moment in music history, took place 57 years ago in August 1969.

angles.name: Woodstock Flashback
angles.copy: 57 years ago, half a million people gathered for three days of peace, love, and music at Woodstock. An unforgettable cultural moment.

events.title: 49 years since the passing of Elvis
events.blurb: August 16, 2026, marks 49 years since the world mourned the death of the King of Rock 'n' Roll, Elvis Presley, who passed away in 1977.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: 49 years ago today, we lost the one and only Elvis Presley. His legacy as the King of Rock 'n' Roll lives on.

events.title: 30 years since Tupac Shakur's death
events.blurb: September 1, 2026, marks 30 years since the tragic death of iconic rapper Tupac Shakur in Las Vegas in 1996.

angles.name: Tupac's Legacy 30 Years On
angles.copy: 30 years ago, the music world lost a giant. Remembering Tupac Shakur and his powerful, enduring legacy today.

events.title: 56 years since Jimi Hendrix's passing
events.blurb: September 2, 2026, marks 56 years since the passing of guitar legend Jimi Hendrix in 1970.

angles.name: Jimi Hendrix Tribute
angles.copy: Remembering the unparalleled genius of Jimi Hendrix, who passed away 56 years ago today. His guitar artistry remains unmatched.

events.title: The Beatles' 'Abbey Road' turns 57
events.blurb: The Beatles released their iconic album 'Abbey Road' on September 26, 1969, making it 57 years old this year.

angles.name: Abbey Road Iconic Album
angles.copy: The legendary 'Abbey Road' by The Beatles turns 57 this year! That album cover is just as famous as the music within.

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: The Pretenders' frontwoman, Chrissie Hynde, celebrates her 75th birthday today, born on September 7, 1951.

angles.name: Happy Birthday Chrissie Hynde
angles.copy: Happy 75th Birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! A true rock icon with a voice that still captivates.

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: Oasis frontman Liam Gallagher celebrates his 54th birthday today, born on September 21, 1972.

angles.name: Happy Birthday Liam Gallagher
angles.copy: Happy 54th Birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! Still bringing that rock 'n' roll swagger. As you were.

events.title: John Lennon's 'Imagine' turns 51
events.blurb: John Lennon released his timeless anthem 'Imagine' on September 9, 1971, which turns 51 this year.

angles.name: Imagine Turns 51
angles.copy: 51 years ago, John Lennon gave us 'Imagine' – a song whose message of peace and unity resonates just as strongly today.

events.title: 25 years since the first iPod
events.blurb: Apple released the first iPod on October 23, 2001, marking 25 years since it revolutionised how we listen to music.

angles.name: iPod Revolution
angles.copy: Remember the first iPod? 25 years ago, Apple launched this game-changer, putting '1,000 songs in your pocket' and changing music consumption forever.

events.title: Global James Bond Day
events.blurb: Global James Bond Day is celebrated annually on October 5th, marking the anniversary of the premiere of the first Bond film, 'Dr. No,' in 1962.

angles.name: Happy James Bond Day
angles.copy: Happy Global James Bond Day! Raise a martini (shaken, not stirred) to 007 and over six decades of iconic espionage.

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: Visionary musician John Lennon, born on October 9, 1940, would have celebrated his 86th birthday today.

angles.name: Remembering John Lennon
angles.copy: Remembering the brilliant John Lennon, who would have been 86 today. His music and message of peace continue to inspire generations.
